ui.title: Ask about Vivek
ui.subtitle: Answers instantly · about Vivek
ui.intro: Hi — I'm Vivek's assistant. I can tell you about his experience, skills, projects, and more. What would you like to know?
suggestions: What is Vivek working on now?

assistant.persona: You are the assistant embedded in Vivek Kumar's portfolio. You answer in his third person as a knowledgeable guide to his work.
assistant.scope: Only answer questions about Vivek Kumar's professional work, skills, experience, projects, availability, education and contact details.
assistant.style: Concise, friendly and factual. Use light HTML emphasis where helpful.
assistant.refusal: I can only help with questions related to Vivek Kumar's work and background.

knowledge.answer: Hi there — I'm Vivek's assistant. Ask me about his experience, skills, projects, current work, education, or how to get in touch.

I'm a small assistant built into Vivek's portfolio — no external AI, just a curated knowledge base about his work. Try: “current work”, “tell me about ResConnect”, “his skills”, “achievements”, or “how to contact him”.

Right now Vivek is a Senior Technical Lead (Software Architect track) at Coforge, working for Dnata Travel — architecting an image migration from SQL Server (images stored inside SQL Server) to Azure Blob Storage and designing the supporting APIs on a .NET tech stack.

On his current Dnata Travel engagement (via Coforge), Vivek is architecting a migration of images stored inside SQL Server over to Azure Blob Storage, and designing the supporting .NET APIs — improving scalability and decoupling binary storage from the database.

ResConnect is Emirates' airline agent booking platform — a multi-year program Vivek worked on at Coforge across 6 projects:
• Microservices transformation (Bags, TIBCO→Wasola, data layer)
• Sidecar authentication (Azure AD, JWT)
• Legacy development & support on the monolith
• Monolith modularization
• RBAC access management
• Microfrontend POC
Ask about any of these for details!

Microservices Transformation (2025–26) — incremental decomposition of the legacy booking monolith, across four active modules:
• Ancillary Services — Bags: end-to-end UI + backend.
• TIBCO → Wasola: migrated SOAP integrations to RESTful Wasola services by extracting transformation/orchestration into a dedicated microservice — no big-bang rewrite.
• Data-layer optimisation for query performance.
Stack: Java 17, Spring Boot, React, REST, Oracle, Couchbase.

Authentication — Access Token & Sidecar (2024–25) — centralized auth for the microservices platform. A dedicated access-token validation service is attached to every service via the sidecar pattern, so auth logic isn't duplicated. Requests flow through a Spring Cloud Gateway → Azure AD token validation → Spring Security claims/role enforcement; invalid tokens are blocked with 401 before reaching the service. Stack: Java 17, Spring API Gateway, Azure AD, JWT, Spring Security.

Legacy Development & Enhancement (Aug 2022 – Mar 2025) — feature work and production support on the high-traffic ResConnect monolith (Spring Framework + AngularJS): flight booking, agent workflows and ancillary services, integrating Oracle (RDBMS) and Couchbase (NoSQL), resolving live production issues while keeping the system stable and backward-compatible.

Monolith Modularization (May 2023 – Mar 2025) — refactored the monolith into a modular architecture as the foundation for microservices: separated frontend and backend into independent repositories, introduced modular design with Java 17 / Spring Boot, while retaining a single deployable (fat JAR). Improved code ownership, testability and migration-readiness.

Access Management — RBAC (Jan 2024 – Aug 2024) — a centralized Role-Based Access Control system managing agent permissions across applications. Vivek extended role-location-permission mapping in Couchbase via a Spring Boot (Java 17) authorization microservice, and built a React.js UI for managing roles, permissions and user access — enabling dynamic authorization across services.

UI Modernization — Microfrontend POC (May 2024 – Aug 2024) — a proof of concept modernizing the agent booking UI with a microfrontend architecture: modular UI for independent development and deployment, evaluating integration of AngularJS with React.js to improve frontend scalability, maintainability and team autonomy.

At Hindustan Times (Sep 2010 – Aug 2022), Vivek built content platforms at news scale — 5 projects:
• Auto Crawling & Scraping Platform
• HTS Portal (millions of articles, Solr search)
• NewZZa Mobile Backend
• NMS RSS Delivery & RSS Splitter modules.
Ask about any one for details!

Auto Crawling & Scraping Platform (2017–21, Hindustan Times) — an end-to-end content automation platform of four integrated systems: Configuration Management (per-site rules via CSS selectors & XPath), Crawling & Scraping (Selenium + HTMLUnit), Article Templating, and a Delivery System (FTP & RSS) — supporting the full writer → QA → client review lifecycle. Stack: Java, Spring Boot, Vue.js, Quasar.

NMS — Article Delivery via RSS (2016, Hindustan Times) — a configurable RSS-based content delivery system for external clients. Built a Vue.js + Quasar UI to manage client configurations and RSS mappings, backend APIs to generate dynamic feeds based on delivery rules, and secured the endpoints with Basic Authentication.

NMS — RSS Splitter (2016, Hindustan Times) — a Spring Boot console application that parses and splits RSS feeds into category-based feeds using JDOM, with sources/rules configured in application.yml, scheduled jobs via Spring Scheduler, and per-feed article limits enforced on merge.

NewZZa Mobile Backend (2015, Hindustan Times) — a RESTful backend serving articles and media to mobile apps. Built REST APIs with Spring MVC and Hibernate, integrated Apache Solr (SolrJ) for indexed retrieval, and used MySQL for configuration and metadata — scalable for content retrieval, filtering and mobile-specific use cases.

HTS Portal (2012–14, Hindustan Times) — a large-scale platform managing millions of articles and images across multiple publications with high-volume daily ingestion. Architecture: Spring DI, Struts 2 (MVC), Hibernate ORM, Bootstrap UI; an FTP ingestion pipeline storing content with metadata; Apache Solr for search/indexing; and MySQL query/index tuning for performance.

Adobe InDesign Auto Pagination (2008–10, Thomson Digital) — an XML-driven pagination workflow built from scratch to automate InDesign typesetting for publishers like Elsevier and Pearson (each with their own XML DTDs). Vivek researched the InDesign JavaScript SDK, built XML→XML transformation in Java/XSLT/XPath, and wrote automation scripts that auto-fill and typeset pages 80–90% — auto table/image placement, bookmarking, linking, XML-out and index generation.

Centralised Kiosk Display & Ad Management — a Linux-based kiosk system displaying advertisements on schedule, with an ad wallet (debit on a successful play, refund on failure). Vivek built the Vue.js admin UI and a backend on PHP, Laravel, MySQL, Redis and queues. Deployed at Rajasthan bus terminals and some malls.

Vivek's R&D roots are at Thomson Digital (2007–2010), automating XML-based publishing — Adobe InDesign typesetting via its JavaScript SDK, and XML transformation pipelines in Java, XSLT and XPath for publishers like Elsevier and Pearson.

QuoteAssist is Vivek's in-progress personal AI POC (2025–26) — a platform that ingests travel quote requests from Outlook or web forms, uses an LLM to extract structured fields (with per-field confidence), enforces corporate policies via RAG, generates pricing-ready JSON and drafts email replies. It self-improves: agent corrections become training signals that graduate trusted patterns to auto-send. Stack: Elixir/Phoenix/OTP, Python (LLM/RAG), React + MS Graph API, PostgreSQL.
You can view Vivek's résumé here: 📄 View Resume (PDF). It's also linked in the chat header and on the page.
You can reach Vivek at viveksingh0143@gmail.com or on LinkedIn. He's open to architecture leadership and system-design roles.
Vivek holds a B.Sc. in Computer Science from Sikkim Manipal University and a Diploma in Information Technology from the Board of Technical Education, Uttar Pradesh.

Key highlights:
• TIBCO → Wasola migration — moved SOAP integrations to RESTful services incrementally, with no big-bang rewrite.
• Led a monolith → microservices transformation that shaped the platform's service design.
• Adopted AI tooling for unit-test generation and legacy-code comprehension, improving delivery efficiency.

Vivek's toolkit:
• Architecture: microservices, event-driven, distributed systems, DDD, API gateway, sidecar.
• Backend: Java 17, Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, Hibernate, Struts 2, REST.
• Frontend: AngularJS, React.js, Vue.js, micro-frontends (Module Federation).
• Messaging & Data: Kafka, Solace, Couchbase, Oracle, MySQL, Apache Solr.
• Platform: CI/CD, Jenkins, Git, VAPT/PT, Agile.
• Add-on: Golang, PHP, Phoenix Framework.
Backend: Java 17, Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, Spring MVC, Spring Security, Hibernate, Struts 2 and REST APIs — the core of most of Vivek's platforms.
Frontend: AngularJS, React.js and Vue.js (with Quasar), plus micro-frontend architecture via Module Federation / Webpack. Vivek works full-stack — building UIs as well as the services behind them.
Messaging & Data: Apache Kafka and Solace for event-driven messaging; Couchbase (distributed NoSQL), Oracle and MySQL for storage; Apache Solr for high-performance search indexing.
Beyond his core JVM stack, Vivek also works with Golang, PHP (Laravel) and the Phoenix Framework as add-on skills.

Vivek is a software architect who breaks monoliths into microservices, designs domain-driven service boundaries, event-driven communication (Kafka/Solace), API gateways and sidecar security — building in scalability, fault isolation and independent deployments from the start.

Vivek has 14 projects plus a personal AI POC:
• Personal: QuoteAssist — agentic AI quote-processing platform (Elixir/Phoenix + Python LLM).
• Emirates / Coforge (6): microservices transformation, sidecar auth, legacy dev, modularization, RBAC, microfrontend POC.
• Hindustan Times (5): crawling & scraping platform, HTS Portal, NewZZa backend, RSS delivery, RSS splitter.
• Thomson Digital (2): InDesign auto-pagination, kiosk ad system.
Ask about any one for details!

Vivek's career spans three companies:
• Coforge — Senior Technical Lead / Software Architect (Aug 2022 – Present); Emirates & Dnata Travel.
• Hindustan Times — Senior Software Engineer (2010 – 2022).
• Thomson Digital — Executive, R&D (2007 – 2010).
Vivek has 14+ years of software engineering experience across airline, media and publishing domains.
Vivek sets technical direction and leads teams — guiding 7+ developers and QA engineers, mentoring through design & code reviews, and shaping the service architecture the platform is built on.
Vivek is based in Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh, India.
Vivek has worked across three domains: airline (Emirates & Dnata booking platforms), media (Hindustan Times content systems) and publishing (Thomson Digital typesetting automation).
Vivek is a Senior Technical Lead on the Software Architect track — setting technical direction, designing systems, and building hands-on across backend, frontend and data.

Vivek Kumar is a Senior Technical Lead / Software Architect with 14+ years building large-scale systems across airline, media and publishing domains. He breaks monoliths into microservices, designs how systems communicate, and builds in security and scale from the start — known for setting technical direction and mentoring teams.
Anytime! Ask me anything else about Vivek's work, skills, projects, or how to reach him.
Thanks for stopping by — feel free to reach out to Vivek over email or LinkedIn anytime.
